title: broussonetia zeylanica
intro: Broussonetia zeylanica, also known as the Ceylon fig tree or Indian laurel, is a deciduous plant native to Sri Lanka and parts of Asia. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Broussonetia zeylanica include its potential to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues, and support the digestive system. Broussonetia zeylanica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, making it a versatile remedy in traditional medicine.
